title: Projects
text:
This page contains completed projects from my courses at Lawrence University.






NoteCMSC405: Advanced Data Computing





NOTE: These are private GitHub repos for the course. As such, only members of the course will have access to the GitHub link.

R-Package Presentation
This is a presentation made with Quarto in RStudio. In it, I explore the basics of KPolen, an R package made by Karl Polen for private equity mathemetical computation and portfolio analysis.
View on RPubs



Article Summary Poster
This is a poster created using the package posterdown. It contains a brief summary of the article: The Split-Apply-Combine Strategy for Data Analysis by Hadley Wickham. This was a collaborative project, worked on with Tahana Akram, a fellow student in CMSC405.
View on GitHub View on RPubs



R-Package “pegap”
pegap is a package developed by myself in an effort to cover the gaps in private equity portfolio analysis in R. The name stems from this: “pe” = private equity, “gap” = gap in the field. Currently, the package contains the following functions:

vintage_diversification_score() — scores how concentrated a PE portfolio is across vintage years using HHI
unfunded_liability_schedule() — forecasts future capital calls across a 5-year schedule with a preset drawdown curve
portfolio_plot() — visualises portfolio progress by vintage year as a scatterplot

I intend to update this package post-graduation to include many more functions.
View on GitHub View Package



Project 3 - Text Analysis
This was a collaborative project with Nga Nguyen (Daisy), a fellow student. We aimed to use data visualisation and sentiment analysis to investigate trends within the Harry Potter Series, specifically within the text itself.
View on GitHub



Project 2 - SQL
Working with Binh Vu, a classmate, this project was an introduction to SQL functions. We used SQL Queries, DuckDB, and Visualisation in RStudio to analyze the TidyTuesday survey data on how individuals interpret probabilistic language.
View on GitHub











NoteECON602: Senior Research Experience





Capstone — Effect of Legalising Recreational Marijuana on State Employment
Research Question: Does legalising recreational marijuana have an impact on state-level employment?
Methodology: Difference-in-Differences model using state and year fixed effects
How: Utilised event study graphs, regression tables, and propensity score matching in RStudio
Data: Tidied, wrangled, and processed over 300,000 observations across 25 variables.
Final Dataset included economic, demographic, geographic controls for all 50 states between the years 2000-2022. These included variables such as GDP, Population, Race, and Region.
View Capstone PDF

title: Oliver Reece
text:
I’m a recent Quantitative Economics graduate from Lawrence University and a former 4-year NCAA Varsity Fencer. I built this portfolio to showcase the intersection of my two biggest drivers: analytical rigor and disciplined execution. This site serves as a deep dive into my technical skill-set - featuring predictive machine learning models, financial valuations, and my professional experience.
If you are curious what’s going on behind the curtain, feel free to check out my website’s github repo! 
Website GitHub

title: About Me
text: My name is Oliver Reece. I hold a B.A. in Quantitative Economics, Minor in Statistics & Data Science from Lawrence University. At Lawrence (outside of my studies) I competed on the NCAA Men’s Fencing Team, and held part-time jobs in both the on-campus bar, and in the office of SEAL (Student Engagement, Activities, and Leadership). I have a passion for investing and quantitative research, and as such intend to merge these together into a career as an investment banking analyst or private equity analyst. In these roles I would aspire to make meaningful investments that would lead to the betterment of the world I live in all while developing as an individual, an academic, and a professional.
